original: Nebuchadrezzar II of Babylon lays siege to Jerusalem under Zedekiah's reign. The siege lasts until July 23, 586 BC.
Otho seizes power in Rome, proclaiming himself Emperor, only rules for three months before committing suicide
Sisinnius begins his reign as Pope (dies 20 days later)
One of the last known Maya dated monuments, a stela from the city of Itzimte (modern Guatemala)
Caliph al-Mustaqfi blinded and ousted
Emperor Louis IV of Bavaria gives his wife Margaretha, Holland and Zealand
France signs secret treaty with German Protestants
Russia cedes Livonia & Estonia to Poland, loses access to Baltic
Battle at Boxum: Spanish troops under Tassis beat state army
French King Henri IV grants military captain Pierre Chauvin de Tonnetuit a ten-year commission for the fur trade in New France - he goes on to found doomed colony of Tadoussac
Tobias Smollett publishes pamphlet accusing Henry Fielding of plagiarism
Riot at burial of Orangist leader Daniel Raap in Amsterdam
Fraunces Tavern opens in NYC
People of New Connecticut (Vermont) declare independence from England
American Continental Congress establishes court of appeals
The Asiatic Society is founded by William Jones at a meeting in Calcutta's Fort William, India
War of 1812: the USS President, an American frigate, is captured by four British frigates
Greek War of Independence: Demetrius Ypsilanti is elected president of the legislative assembly.
1st US railroad honeymoon trip, Mr & Mrs Pierson, Charleston, South Carolina
1st US-built locomotive to pull a passenger train makes 1st run
HMS Beagle anchors at Goeree Tierra del Fuego
University of Notre Dame receives its charter in Indiana
1st Swedish magazine in US, Skandinavia, published in NYC
General Mariano Arista replaces Mexican President José Joaquín de Herrera
1st first-class game in Sydney, NSW v Vic at The Domain
US Civil War: Union forces at Fort Pikens on Santa Rosa Island near Pensacola, Florida refuse Florida Militia demand to surrender
1st US newspaper printed on wood-pulp paper, Boston Morning Journal
Second Battle of Fort Fisher: Union Navy bombards Fort Fisher, North Carolina, allowing Union troops to capture Confederacy's last remaining seaport
"Die Afrikaanse Patriot", the first newspaper in Afrikaans, is published in Paarl
US Assay Office in Helena, Montana opens
1st US ski club forms (Berlin NH)
French explorer Jules Nicholas Crevaux is the first European to discover an Inca City near Sarta, north-west Argentina
Weekly Herald, 1st newspaper in Vancouver, BC, publishes 1st issue
Australian cricket spin bowler Albert Trott takes 8-43 on debut to end England's 2nd innings at 143 in 3rd Test in Adelaide; Australia wins by 382 in 4 days
French fleet reaches Majunga, Madagascar
Henry Arthur Jones' "Michael & his Lost Angel" premieres in London
3-element vacuum tube patented by American inventor Lee De Forest
Gold dental inlays first described by William Taggart, who invented them
Alpha Kappa Alpha sorority, the first for African-American women, established by Ethel Hedgeman and 15 other students at Howard University in Washington, D.C.
C Hill & R J Hartigan make 8th wkt partnership 243 for Australia
Constructions ends on the Buffalo Bill Dam in Wyoming
1st airplane bombing experiments with explosives at San Francisco
Japan claims economic control of China
Rosa Luxemburg and Karl Liebknecht, two of the most prominent socialists in Germany, are tortured and murdered by the Freikorps
Semana Tragica (Tragic Week): Bloodbath in Buenos Aires
Two million gallons of molasses flood Boston Massachusetts in the "Great Molasses Flood" when a storage tank burst, drowning 21 and injuring 150
3rd Dutch government Ruijs de Beerenbrouck forms
Record snowfall of 2 inches in city of Los Angeles, California
8.4 earthquake in India/Nepal, 10,700 die
300 Dutch ice cream salesmen protest against Italian competition
1st all-glass windowless structure in US completed, Toledo, Ohio
Horace Stoneham is elected president of New York Giants MLB franchise; succeeds late father, Charles; remains president for next 40 years, presiding over move to SF, before selling team in 1976
Nonprofit Ford Foundation incorporates
Spanish Civil War: Second Battle of the Corunna Road ends after both forces withdraw
Municipal Railway & Market St RR begin service to Transbay Terminal
German U-Boot torpedoes Dutch merchant ship Arendskerk (Eagle's Church)
Chicago Cubs drop plans to install lights at Wrigley Field because of military's need for materials; takes 35 years before lights finally installed at the venerable ballpark
1,000 workers complete air conditioning system for Pentagon
1st transport of Jews from Amsterdam to concentration camp Vught
World War II: The Soviets begin a counter-offensive at Voronezh
World's largest office building, the Pentagon is completed to house the US military
European Advisory Commission decides to divide Germany
Vught Concentration Camp puts 74 women in 1 cell, 10 die
Every Amsterdammer gets 3 kg of sugar beets
Red Army frees Crakow-Plaszow concentration camp
Mao's Red army conquers Ten-tsin
4,000 attend National Emergency Civil Rights Conference in Washington, D.C.
"Cloud of Death" rolls down Mount Lamington, New Guinea kills 3-5,000
Ilse Koch, also known as "The Bitch of Buchenwald", is sentenced to life imprisonment by a West German court
Supreme Court rule "clear & present danger" of incitement to riot is not protected speech & can be a cause for arrest
16 car Federal Express train loses brakes & crashes in Washington, D.C. station

East Germany's first Minister of Foreign Affairs, Georg Dertingen arrested for "espionage", beginning a purge of senior officials, accused of spying for 'imperialistic powers' and plotting against the state
1st official act of Princess Beatrice, launches tanker Vasum
USSR ends state of war with German Federal Republic
Dmitri Shostakovichappointed honorary member of Academia Santa Cecilia
KWAB TV channel 4 in Big Spring, TX (NBC) begins broadcasting
Brooklyn Dodgers extend their 5-year lease on Ebbets Field by signing a new 3-year lease with real estate developer Marvin Kratter; club moves to Los Angeles the next year
New York Yankees announce that 140 MLB games to be televised on WPIX TV this season in a deal worth over $1 million dollars

Battle of Arafura Sea: Dutch destroyers intercept and destroy three Indonesian torpedo boats attempting to land marines on the territory of Netherlands New Guinea as part of the Soviet-Indonesian Operation Trikora
Europe's oldest surviving manuscript, the Derveni Papyrus, a commentary on an Orphic poem from c. 340 BC, is rediscovered in a tomb in Derveni, Northern Greece
MLB executives vote to hold a free agent amateur draft, officially known as Major League Baseball First-Year Player Draft in NYC
Teamsters negotiate 1st national labor contract
Brunswick Records releases the single "I Can't Explain" by British rockers "The Who" in the UK, their 1st after changing their band name
Soviet underground nuclear test creates the atomic lake Chagan, Kazakhstan
Washtenaw Community College in Ann Arbor, Michigan, forms
The First Nigerian Republic is overthrown in a military coup d'etat by Kaduna Nzeogwu and Emmanuel Ifeajuna, the Prime Minister of Nigeria and other senior political and military figures are killed
Super Bowl I, LA Memorial Coliseum, Los Angeles, CA: Green Bay Packers beat KC Chiefs, 35-10; MVP: Bart Starr, Green Bay, QB
KDCD TV channel 18 in Midland, Texas (IND) begins broadcasting
Minnesota North Stars forward Bill Masterton dies 2 days after suffering a head injury in a game against the Oakland Seals; the only death as a result of a game injury in NHL history
Nuclear test at Pacific Ocean
Soyuz 5 launched by Soviet Union
Nigerian civil war officially ends after 2 1/2 years of fighting when the Republic of Biafra disbands and joins Nigeria
Riots break out in the Ardoyne area of Belfast
Aswan High Dam officially opens in Egypt
TV drama "Emergency" with Randolph Mantooth and Robert Fuller premieres on NBC TV
4 Watergate burglars plead guilty in federal court
Movie critic Gene Shalit joins Today Show panel
Expert panel reports 18½ minute gap in Watergate tape, 5 separate erasures
Portugal signs accord for Angola's independence
Space Mountain opens at Disney World in Orlando, Florida
US-German Helios B solar probe launched into solar orbit
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Coneheads debut on "Saturday Night Live"
Festac '77, the Second World Black and African Festival of Arts and Culture, begins in Lagos, Nigeria
Super Bowl XII, Louisiana Superdome, New Orleans, LA: Dallas Cowboys beat Denver Broncos, 27-10; MVP: Harvey Martin, Dallas, DE & Randy White, Dallas, DT
Theodore Bundy kills Fla State U coeds Lisa Levy & Margaret Bowman
Pam Gems' "Piaf!" premieres in London
Pitcher Bob Gibson is elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame in his first year of eligibility; after a stellar 17 year career, polls 337 votes from Baseball Writers Association of America
Police drama "Hill Street Blues" premieres on NBC-TV
"Forbidden Broadway" by/with Gerard Alessandrini premieres in NYC
Mark Thatcher, son of UK prime minister Margaret Thatcher, makes his way home after 6 days missing in the Sahara
Dutch political party DS'70 disbands
Javed Miandad& Mudassar Nazar make 451 stand v India
Thom Syles keeps a life saver intact in his mouth for over 7 hours
Schonbrun skates world record 5 km (7:39.44)
Tony Benn, described as a “left wing rebel” wins Labour's nomination for the by-election in Chesterfield, England
Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded to John Ashbery & Fred Chapell
CBS' premiere of TV made teen drug abuse drama, "Not My Kid", based on the previous year's book by Beth Polson
Tancredo Neves becomes 1st elected President of Brazil in 21 years, but dies before taking office
Living Seas opens (Disneyland)
Police officer who mistakenly shot and paralysed an innocent woman in Brixton, UK, is cleared of all criminal charges

19-year-old Indian cricket leg-spinner Narenda Hirwani records best bowling figures on debut in Test history; captures 16-136 in 255 run 4th Test win v West Indies in Madras; Hirwani takes 8 wickets in each innings
Indian cricket wicketkeeper Kiran More stumps 5 West Indian batsman in 4th Test at Madras; world Test record
Narendra Hirwani takes 16-136 (8-61 & 8-75) v WI on Test debut
NFL analyst Jimmy "The Greek" Snyder makes several questionable comments about African Americans during a lunchtime interview on CBS' NFL Today; fired the next day
Cerebral Palsy telethon raises $22.6 million
AT&T experiences long distance problems due to a computer glitch
Golden State coach Don Nelson becomes 2nd man in NBA history to appear in 1,000 games both as a player and coach (joining Lenny Wilkens) as the Warriors lose to the Indiana Pacers, 144-105
In a bizarre twist in the NHL, Washington Capitals fire coach Brian Murray, who had been head coach since 1981; replace him with his brother Terry, who had been coaching in the minors
UN's deadline for Iraq to withdraw from Kuwait (they don't)
Bulgaria recognizes Macedonia
Cleaning woman finds intimate photos of Sarah Ferguson with US man
Supreme Court rules 5-3 that Joseph Doherty isn't entitled to asylum
7.5 earthquake strikes northern Japan, killing 2 people
Soap opera "Santa Barbara" final show on NBC TV
Top Italian mafia leader Salvatore "Toto" Riina arrested in Palermo, Sicily
Dallas Mavericks drop a 104-87 decision to the San Antonio Spurs at Reunion Arena to set an unwanted NBA record with their 17th consecutive home defeat; break mark of 16 set by Orlando in 1990
Hague motorist with .51% alcohol in blood, breaks Dutch record (.47%)
Queen Elizabeth falls off her horse & breaks her left wrist
Southern Alabama begins using new area code 334
Western Washington begins using new area code 360
San Francisco head coach George Seifert announces his resignation after 8 highly-successful NFL seasons with the 49ers: winningest coach in team history at 108-35; 2 Super Bowl titles

Seattle's Sam Perkins ties Jeff Hornacek's NBA single-game record by connecting on 8 straight 3-point field goals during Sonics' 122-78 win over visiting Toronto; Seattle also sets an NBA steals record with 27
Space Shuttle Atlantis docks with Mir Space Station

Australian schoolboy Ian Thorpe becomes youngest ever male to become a world swimming champion when he wins 400m freestyle aged 15 years, 3 months in Perth; also wins event at 2001 & 2003 World Championships
KingHenry VIIIdeclares himself head of the Church in England
KingFrancis I of Francecommissions Jean-Francois Roberval to settle the province of New France
Elizabeth Icrowned Queen of England in Westminster Abbey, London
Voltaire's satire "Candide" is published anonymously in five editions in five countries to scandalous acclaim (estimated date)
Robert Morris, Superintendent of Finance, recommends to U.S. Congress establishment of decimal coinage and a national mint
Russian writerFyodor Dostoevsky's first novel "Poor Folk" is published in the St Petersburg Collection almanac
Steam elevator patented byElisha Otis
Basketball rules first published in Triangle Magazine, written byJames Naismith
AbdulazizIbn Saudleads 40 men over the walls of Riyadh and takes the city, marking the beginning of the Third Saudi State
Frank Wedekind's play "Die letzten Tage der Menschheit (The Last Days of Mankind)" premieres
Hans Lutherforms German government, with DNVP
Brilliant West Indian cricket batsmanGeorge Headleyscores 157 of 176 on debut on the 4th day of the drawn 1st Test against England in Bridgetown, Barbados
New York Yankees sluggerBabe Ruth, nearing end of illustrious career, signs a 1-year contract worth $35,000; represents a pay cut of $17,000 for "The Babe"
While robbing the First National Bank in East Chicago, Indianapolis,John Dillingeris shot several times by officer William O'Malley, but survives because he is wearing a bullet proof vest.
Clifford Odets' first produced play "Waiting for Lefty" premieres in NYC
US President Franklin Delano Roosevelt sends his famed "Green Light Letter" to MLB CommissionerKenesaw Mountain Landis, encouraging baseball to continue playing during World War II
GeneralEisenhowerarrives in England to take up his command as Supreme Commander of the Allied Expeditionary Force
The brutalized corpse ofElizabeth Short("TheBlack Dahlia") found in Leimert Park, Los Angeles, California
"The Treasure of the Sierra Madre", film based on B. Traven's novel, directed by John Huston and starringHumphrey Bogartpremieres
Red Wings' rookie goalieTerry Sawchukrecords his first of 115 career NHL shutouts, as Detroit beats the New York Rangers, 1-0 at Madison Square Garden
Dmitri Shostakovich's "From Jewish Folk Poetry" premieres in Leningrad
Berry GordysignsThe Supremesto Motown Records
Coco the Clown[Nicolai Poliakoff] is the subject of the BBC TV programme "This is Your Life"
San Francisco Giants make champion outfielderWillie Maysthe highest-paid player in baseball when they sign him to a new $105,000 per season contract
One of the biggest trades in NBA history; San Francisco Warriors sendWilt Chamberlainto the Philadelphia 76ers for Connie Dierking, Lee Shaffer, Paul Neumann and cash
Rolling Stones appear for the fifth time on theEd SullivanShow, reluctantly agree to alter lyrics of "Let's Spend The Night Together"
Prime Minister of Northern IrelandTerence O'Neillannounces that an official inquiry will analyse the 'troubles' in Northern Ireland
Muammar Gaddafiis proclaimed Chairman of Libyan Revolutionary Command Council - de facto head of state
George Harrisonreleases 1st solo single "My Sweet Lord" in the UK; tops the charts in 15 countries, and becomes subject of a copyright infringement lawsuit
World heavyweight boxing championJoe Frazierstops Terry Daniels in 4 rounds in New Orleans to retain his WBC and WBA titles
PopePaul VIholds an audience with Israel Prime MinisterGolda Meirat the Vatican
US PresidentRichard Nixonsuspends all US offensive action in North Vietnam
TV sitcom "Happy Days" created byGarry Marshalland starring Ron Howard,Henry Winkler, Marion Ross, and Tom Bosley, begins an 11 year run on ABC
Pioneering American female comedianPhyllis Dillergets a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
Sara Jane Moore sentenced to life for attempting to shoot US PresidentGerald Ford
PopeJohn Paul IIreceivesLech Wałęsaand Solidarity delegation at the Vatican
John McEnroereverses previous year's result with a 6–3, 6–4, 6–4 win overIvan Lendlto claim his second season-ending ATP Masters Grand Prix tennis title at Madison Square Garden, NYC

Martina Navratilova’s 54-match winning streak ends when beaten by Hana Mandlíková 7-6, 3-6, 6-4 in the final of the Virginia Slims of California tennis event in Oakland; after loss Navratilova wins next 74 matches for new record
Montreal's rookie goaltenderPatrick Royrecords his first of 66 career NHL shutouts, in the Canadiens' 4-0 win against the Winnipeg Jets at the Forum
Champion Boston Celtics NBA forwardLarry Birdis named Man of the Year by 'The Sporting News' and Athlete of the Year by 'Associated Press'; first time one athlete gains both distinctions
42-year-old former world heavyweight boxing championGeorge Foremanstops Gerry Cooney in 2 rounds in Atlantic City in a non-title bout; Cooney's last fight
In a shrewd move, Detroit Tigers sign 1st basemanCecil Fielderwho spent previous season in Japan; hits 51 homers this MLB season and becomes a premier power hitter for most of the 1990s
Elizabeth IIsigns letters patent that allows Australia to institute its own Victoria Cross, the first Commonwealth realm to do so

7th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: Bobby "Blue" Bland; Booker T. and the MG's;Johnny Cash;Jimi HendrixExperience; Isley Brothers; Sam and Dave; The Yardbirds; Elmore James; Professor Longhair; Leo Fender; Bill Graham; and Doc Pomus
Lawrence Taylorannounces his retirement from the NFL

Controversial Chicago Bulls' forwardDennis Rodmankicks cameraman Eugene Amos in the groin during a game against Minnesota Timberwolves; ultimately pays Amos $200,000 settlement, and is suspended for 11 games without pay
Diana, Princess of Wales, calls for an international ban on landmines, angering ministers in the UK
"Louis Theroux's Weird Weekends" documentary premieres on BBC2
Future Hockey Hall of Fame right wingDino Ciccarellifinds his 5th NHL club; traded from Tampa Bay Lightning to Florida Panthers
British Museum opens in Montague House, London
1st top hat worn by John Etherington of London
Donkey first used as symbol of Democratic Party, in Harper's Weekly

Arthur Griffithis elected president of the Irish Free State after Eamon de Valera resigns in opposition to the Anglo-Irish Treaty (De Valera will lead a military opposition seeking a unified and independent Ireland)
Tennessee Supreme Court overturns (on a technicality)John T. Scopes' guilty verdict for teaching evolution in the “Scopes monkey trial”, but the law itself remains in force
The Manhattan Project's G-5 Group, headed by Physicist's Donald Kerst and Seth Neddermeyer, take their first betatron pictures of a nuclear implosion at the Los Alamos Laboratory
